methodology: Municipal product OKRs with a small set of company objectives, outcome-oriented key results, and explicit team ownership across field, evidence, workflow, and insight product areas.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe municipal outcomes and operating leverage, not internal feature shipping alone.
Key results focus on incident reduction, case throughput, cleanup speed, and compliance confidence.
Every key result assigns executing and supporting teams so shared operational ownership stays explicit.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Make waste-enforcement deployments produce visible and enforceable outcomes faster
companyObjectives.statement: Improve hotspot planning, case throughput, and field coordination so councils can show cleaner spaces and stronger enforcement within one budget cycle.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Hotspot proof depends on visible action, not only device rollout
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows that municipal credibility comes from connecting deployment, action, and outcome proof.

objectives.title: 2026-Q2 Increase enforceable case throughput without adding officer paperwork
objectives.objective: Turn more validated incidents into legally usable cases while reducing the manual effort required from municipal police and operators.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Qualified incidents converted into enforceable case files.
Average officer handling time per validated incident.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Administrative effort required to assemble and progress a case.
Annualized fines recovered from instrumented enforcement cases.

keyResults.target: Increase monthly enforceable case throughput in mature deployments
keyResults.baseline: Too many qualified incidents still stop in review or require procedural rework before sanction.
keyResults.statement: Increase case throughput by improving triage quality, evidence packaging, and guided case progression inside the default operator workflow.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve qualified incident quality and evidence readiness.
Lead the workflow and procedural changes that turn more incidents into enforceable case files.

supportingTeams.intent: Support partner handoff and treasury connectivity needed to close the loop.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is central to municipal proof and should behave like a delivery commitment with explicit execution ownership.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.target: Increase monthly enforceable case throughput in mature deployments
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: case throughput rises materially as triage and guided case flow remove avoidable drop-off.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Increase monthly enforceable case throughput in mature deployments
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: case throughput ends the year sustainably ahead of today's baseline with less procedural waste.

keyResults.target: Reduce average officer handling time per validated incident
keyResults.baseline: Operators and officers still spend too much time assembling evidence, preparing documents, and repeating status updates.
keyResults.statement: Reduce officer time per incident by making evidence access, document generation, and partner handoff happen inside one guided workflow.

executingTeams.intent: Lead case and document workflow improvements that remove manual effort.

supportingTeams.intent: Support stronger evidence retrieval and governed access.
Support clean field closure and less follow-up rework.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should improve through product-quality iteration and field feedback rather than deadline-only pressure.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.target: Reduce average officer handling time per validated incident
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: officer handling time drops meaningfully as more of the case flow becomes structured and reusable.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Reduce average officer handling time per validated incident
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: officer handling time remains sustainably below today's baseline across more municipalities.

objectives.title: 2026-Q2 Connect enforcement and cleanup into one recurrence-reducing loop
objectives.objective: Make validated incidents lead to faster field action and better recurrence prevention so councils see cleaner spaces and service teams waste fewer trips.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Median time from validated incident to completed cleanup.
Share of treated hotspots that recur within 30 days.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Operational leverage from lower repeat visits and less fragmented coordination.
Combined council value from cleaner spaces, lower cost, and higher recoveries.

keyResults.target: Reduce cleanup cycle time from validated incident to completed intervention
keyResults.baseline: Service teams still receive too many low-context tasks and spend time coordinating status across separate tools.
keyResults.statement: Reduce cleanup cycle time by connecting validated incidents, structured dispatch, mobile closure, and recurrence-aware prioritization in one workflow.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the dispatch and mobile workflow improvements that speed field execution.

supportingTeams.intent: Support service-system connectivity and closure data exchange.
Support recurring hotspot analytics and operational value measurement.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is critical to proving that the platform improves municipal service operations, not only enforcement workflow.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.target: Reduce cleanup cycle time from validated incident to completed intervention
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: cleanup cycle time drops materially as dispatch quality and mobile closure improve.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Reduce cleanup cycle time from validated incident to completed intervention
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: cleanup cycle time remains sustainably below today's baseline across the larger installed base.

keyResults.baseline: Teams still struggle to connect deployment placement, field intervention, and prevention decisions into one recurrence-aware loop.
keyResults.statement: Reduce hotspot recurrence by improving recurrence analytics, relocation guidance, and the quality of service and enforcement follow-up.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the recurrence and benchmark analytics needed to guide hotspot decisions.
Lead the deployment and relocation decisions informed by recurrence evidence.

supportingTeams.intent: Support higher-quality intervention data and closure signals.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR depends on evidence quality and operational learning over time, so it should improve through iterative product evidence.

target1.label: 2026-Q3 target
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: recurrence falls on a subset of mature sites but broader consistency depends on better relocation decisions.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: recurrence should end the year clearly lower than today's baseline if recurrence-aware operating loops become standard.
